article_title: Etsy Completes Five-Year Migration of Its 1,000-Shard, 425 TB MySQL Architecture to Vitess

content_preview.title: Etsy Completes Five-Year Migration of Its 1,000-Shard, 425 TB MySQL Architecture to Vitess
content_preview.summary: Etsy has finished migrating its proprietary 1,000-shard MySQL infrastructure to Vitess after five years, 2,500 pull requests, and 6,000 query rewrites, eliminating a single point of failure while preserving its existing data layout through custom vindexes.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Etsy has completed a migration that took five years, roughly 2,500 pull requests, and the rewriting of approximately 6,000 database queries: moving shard routing for its entire production MySQL estate from a proprietary in-house system to Vitess, the open-source database clustering project that [graduated from the Cloud Native Computing Foundation in 2019](https://www.cncf.io/announcements/2019/11/05/cloud-native-computing-foundation-announces-vitess-graduation/). The e-commerce mar...

editor_notes.source_verification: InfoQ VERIFIED via WebFetch: 5-year migration, 1000 shards, 425 TB, 2500 PRs, 6000 query rewrites, 1.7M req/s, Ella Yarmo-Gray quote, custom vindexes approach all confirmed.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All migration metrics verified against InfoQ primary source.
editor_notes.originality: No prior Etsy or Vitess migration coverage.
